prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Find the branch starting with `m4/` (run `git branch -a | grep 'm4/'`). There ma

risks_fired.evidence: Three cp-command lines embed `<repo-name>` angle-bracket placeholders: `../<repo-name>-m5`, `cp ../<repo-name>/CLAUDE.local.md ../<repo-name>-m5/`, `cp -r ../<repo-name>/.claude/memory/ ../<repo-name>-m5/.claude/memory/`. Claude Code can likely infer repo name from CWD but may substitute the literal angle-bracket string or pause to confirm; a wrong-path cp with `(if it exists)` suffix fails silently, leaving M5/M6 compounding without M1/M3 personal rules — silent, no error surface. Personal-scope integrate-verb carve-out does NOT apply: these are cp shell commands, not integrate/merge/reshape verbs.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Replace `<repo-name>` placeholders with self-resolution inside the fence: 'Determine the repo name from `basename $(pwd)` and substitute it in all paths below' — removes placeholders while keeping the prompt self-contained.

risks_fired.evidence: Prompt chains four distinct sub-steps (find branch, create worktree, copy files, report) with no `No preamble` suppressor and no body primer immediately after the fence naming the preamble + skim-past cue. Rule 17 structural trigger fires: 4+ named sub-steps require one of: (a) in-fence `No preamble. <first action>` suppressor; (b) body primer right after fence with preamble + skim cue. The post-fence body callout addresses multi-branch grep disambiguation, not preamble behavior.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add a body primer immediately after the fence: 'Claude will narrate before acting; look for the shell command output confirming the worktree path and which files copied across.' No prompt edit required.

risks_fired.evidence: New version bakes anti-softening structurally into the fence: `no narrative, no hedging`, `did NOT ship — each item, no "partial" / "mostly" / "in progress"`. Body callout after fence explicitly names RLHF softening risk and gives recovery move. §50 convenience-bias carve-out applies: in-fence enforcement + body callout together neutralise. risks_fired.evidence: `verifier — last output line of each run, pasted verbatim` forces literal artifact evidence rather than Claude's summary.
